one_line_summary: Likely mouse ortholog of human VWA7 (von Willebrand A domain-containing protein 7), a putative phosphatidylcholine-specific phospholipase C (pc-PLC) with structural homology to bacterial pc-PLC active site; poorly characterized.
Predicted mouse gene with no characterized protein function; abstracts are exclusively about C. elegans epithelial junction proteins (PAR-3, DLG-1, AJM-1) with no clear ortholog mapping to Gm996.

neural_phenotype: neurodevelopmental deficits (intellectual disability, hypotonia, seizures, sensorineural hearing loss, cleft palate when disrupted by chromosomal translocation at 11p15.4)
neuro_disease: neurodevelopmental disorder (MIM 140750); disruption associated with severe intellectual disability, seizures, muscular hypotonia, sensorineural hearing loss
one_line_summary: ST5 (suppression of tumorigenicity 5) at 11p15.4 functions as a tumor suppressor and scaffold; disruption causes severe neurodevelopmental syndrome with intellectual disability, hypotonia, seizures, and sensorineural hearing loss.

molecular_action: antiviral restriction; inhibition of ribosomal frameshifting
pathways: innate immune/interferon response; antiviral restriction; RNA stability regulation
one_line_summary: Mouse ortholog of human SHFL (Shiftless/C19orf66); an interferon-stimulated gene that restricts replication of multiple RNA viruses (flaviviruses, HCV, coronaviruses, enteroviruses) by inhibiting programmed ribosomal frameshifting and destabilizing viral RNA; highly expressed in hepatocytes basally.

pathways: Hedgehog signaling; p53 pathway; cellular senescence; DNA damage response
cancer_role: tumor suppressor (transcriptionally induced by p53/DNA damage; downregulated in gastric cancer; antisense lncRNA PTCHD4-AS also tumor-suppressive); mRNA stabilized by m6A in senescent cells
one_line_summary: Mouse ortholog of human PTCHD4 (Patched domain-containing protein 4 / PTCH53); a p53 target gene homologous to PTCH1 that represses Hedgehog signaling; downregulated in gastric cancer; mRNA undergoes m6A modification during senescence.

ko_phenotype: enlarged lymph nodes; thrombocytosis; decreased mean corpuscular hemoglobin (MGI)
cancer_role: prognostic marker (appears in cancer prognosis gene signatures)
differentiation_role: hematopoietic differentiation (KO affects platelet number and erythrocyte hemoglobin)
one_line_summary: Poorly characterized RIKEN gene; MGI knockout shows hematopoietic phenotype (thrombocytosis, enlarged lymph nodes, low MCH), suggesting a role in blood cell homeostasis; no neural function established.

pathways: innate immunity; ECM remodeling; neuroinflammation (inferred from Chi3l family)
neuro_disease: neuroinflammation (CHI3L1/YKL-40 family implicated in neurodegeneration and glioma; Chi3l7 is a related family member)
one_line_summary: Mouse chitinase 3-like 7; member of the chitinase/chitinase-like protein family (YKL-40/CHI3L1 subfamily); likely lacks catalytic chitinase activity but may bind chitin/glycosaminoglycans; family members are markers of neuroinflammation and glioma, but Chi3l7 itself is poorly characterized.

cancer_role: candidate tumor suppressor (located in minimally deleted region on 12p in myeloid malignancies; altered in AML Golgi gene network; plasma protein levels changed in CRC)
one_line_summary: MANSC domain-containing protein 1; contains MANSC motif (N-terminal seven-cysteine domain); resides in one of only 9 genes in the minimal 12p deletion region found in myeloid malignancies, suggesting candidate tumor suppressor role; poorly functionally characterized.

cancer_types: lung cancer; prostate cancer; breast cancer; endometrial cancer
cancer_role: prognostic marker in multiple cancer types
one_line_summary: Mouse ortholog of human C9orf152 (chromosome 9 open reading frame 152); highly expressed in reproductive tract (uterus/oviduct); appears as prognostic gene in multiple cancer expression signatures; molecular function unknown.
